title: Four Thousand Weeks: Time Management for Mortals
subtitle: Embrace your limits. Change your life.
author: Oliver Burkeman
authorBio: Oliver Burkeman (b. 1975) is a British author and journalist. He wrote the popular weekly psychology column 'This Column Will Change Your Life' for The Guardian for over a decade. His work focuses on the intersection of psychology, philosophy, productivity, and happiness — with a skeptical, humane eye toward self-help culture. He is also the author of The Antidote: Happiness for People Who Can't Stand Positive Thinking and Meditations for Mortals.
excerpt: The average human lifespan is absurdly, terrifyingly, insultingly short. Assuming you live to be eighty, you will have had about four thousand weeks. Nobody needs telling that there isn't enough time. We're obsessed by our lengthening to-do lists, our overfilled inboxes, the struggle against distraction, and the sense that we ought to be getting more done. But what if the problem isn't that we're not managing our time well enough — but that the entire modern obsession with productivity is a trap? Four Thousand Weeks is a philosophical inquiry into the brevity of life, a radical reframing of what it means to use time well. Rather than offering yet another system for getting more done, Burkeman argues that embracing our limits — accepting finitude — is the only path to a meaningful, fulfilling life.
